;<span id="Element">element</span>
:single page of information stored in [[Glossary:SuperMemo|SuperMemo]] (e.g. an article, a question-answer pair, etc.). All '''elements''' kept together are called a [[Glossary:Collection|collection]]. '''Elements''' may have the form of [[Glossary:Topic|topics]] (articles, [[Glossary:Extract|extracts]], summaries, etc.), [[Glossary:Item|items]] (testing material), [[Glossary:Concept|concepts]] (general ideas used in semantic learning), or [[Glossary:Task|tasks]] ('''elements''' representing to-do jobs). A [[Glossary:Topic|topic]] presents a larger part of the learning material, e.g. an article about the greenhouse effect. [[Glossary:Item|Items]] provide specific testing questions, e.g. ''How thick is the cerebral cortex?'' In the simplest case, [[Glossary:Topic|topics]] have the form of a page of text while [[Glossary:Item|items]] are formulated as questions and answers (see: [[Items, topics, concepts, and tasks|Topics vs. Items]]). Every '''element''' is represented in the [[Glossary:Contents_window|'''Contents''' window]] as a single [[Glossary:Leaf|leaf]] or branch of the [[Glossary:Knowledge_tree|knowledge tree]]. The content of individual '''elements''' is displayed in the [[Glossary:Element_window|element window]]. Read more: [[Items, topics, concepts, and tasks]]
